legal.draftBanner: This Czech translation is a draft provided for convenience. The English version of this document is the legally authoritative text; in case of any discrepancy, the English version prevails.

changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via the platform or by email before they take effect. The "Last updated" date at the top reflects the most recent revision.
changes.heading: 10. Changes to This Policy

cookies.body: We use strictly necessary cookies to maintain your authenticated session (iron-session cookie). With your consent we also set analytics cookies to measure how you use the platform and marketing cookies to record consent preferences. You can manage your cookie choices at any time via the cookie settings link in the footer.
cookies.heading: 5. Cookies

dataWeCollect.body: We collect data you provide directly (name, email, password hash, display name), data generated by your use of the service (audit logs, billing records, notification history, files you upload), and, with your consent, analytics data (page views, feature usage) and marketing data (click-through records for communications we send).
dataWeCollect.heading: 2. Data We Collect

howWeUse.body: Your data is used to operate and improve the platform, to send transactional notifications (account activity, billing receipts), to fulfil legal obligations, and — with your consent — to analyse usage patterns and to send marketing communications. We do not sell your personal data to third parties.
howWeUse.heading: 4. How We Use Your Data

legalBasis.body: We process your personal data on the following bases: contract performance (to provide the service you signed up for), legal obligation (audit retention, AML), legitimate interest (security monitoring, fraud prevention), and consent (analytics, marketing, third-party sharing — you can change these at any time in Privacy settings).
legalBasis.heading: 3. Legal Basis for Processing

processors.body: We use Stripe for payment processing (subject to Stripe's privacy policy). Where enabled by your tenant, we may share data with other processors listed in the platform settings. We enter into data processing agreements with all sub-processors and require them to apply equivalent protections.
processors.heading: 9. Third-Party Processors

retention.body: We retain your personal data for as long as your account is active. Audit and billing records are retained for the minimum period required by applicable law (typically 7–10 years) even after account deletion; personal identifiers in those records are anonymised on erasure. All other data is deleted within 30 days of account closure.
retention.heading: 6. Data Retention

security.body: Passwords are hashed using Argon2. Sensitive personal data fields are encrypted at rest (AES-GCM) under per-subject encryption keys. TLS is enforced in transit. Access to personal data is restricted to the data subject and platform administrators via role-based permissions and Postgres row-level security.
security.heading: 8. Security

whoWeAre.body: ModularPlatform is a SaaS platform operated as a data controller under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). References to "we", "us", or "our" mean the platform operator. If you have questions about this policy, contact us via the support channel in your account dashboard.
whoWeAre.heading: 1. Who We Are

yourRights.body: Under GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, and port your personal data, and to object to processing. You can exercise most rights directly in the Privacy settings page. To download all data we hold about you, use the "Download my data" button. To permanently delete your account and erase personal data, use the "Delete my account" option — this crypto-shreds your encryption key so stored data becomes permanently unrecoverable.
yourRights.heading: 7. Your Rights

acceptableUse.body: You agree not to use the platform to transmit unlawful content, attempt to circumvent security controls, reverse-engineer any component of the service, or interfere with the availability of the service for other users.
acceptableUse.heading: 4. Acceptable Use

acceptance.body: By creating an account or using ModularPlatform you agree to these Terms of Service and our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>. If you do not agree, do not use the service.
acceptance.heading: 1. Acceptance of Terms

accounts.body: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and for all activity that occurs under your account. You must notify us immediately of any unauthorised use. We may suspend accounts that violate these terms or that we believe are compromised.
accounts.heading: 3. Accounts and Security

billing.body: Credit purchases are processed by Stripe and are non-refundable unless required by applicable law. Unused credits expire according to the plan terms visible in your billing dashboard.
billing.heading: 6. Billing and Credits

changes.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Material changes will be notified via the platform or by email. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
changes.heading: 9. Changes to These Terms

description.body: ModularPlatform is a multi-tenant SaaS platform that provides identity management, billing, file storage, notifications, and GDPR tooling via a modular architecture. Access to individual modules depends on the entitlements granted to your tenant.
description.heading: 2. Description of Service

governingLaw.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Czech Republic. Disputes shall be resolved in the courts of the Czech Republic unless mandatory consumer-protection law in your jurisdiction provides otherwise.
governingLaw.heading: 10. Governing Law

intellectualProperty.body: All platform software and documentation are the exclusive property of ModularPlatform and its licensors. Your data remains yours; you grant us a limited licence to process it solely to provide the service.
intellectualProperty.heading: 5. Intellectual Property

liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, ModularPlatform is not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service. Our total liability to you shall not exceed the amounts you paid us in the twelve months preceding the claim.
liability.heading: 8. Limitation of Liability

termination.body: You may delete your account at any time via the Privacy settings page. We may terminate or suspend access for violations of these terms. On termination, your data is erased in accordance with our Privacy Policy and applicable GDPR obligations.
termination.heading: 7. Termination

dialog.description: This is irreversible. All personal data will be erased from every module. Audit logs will be anonymised and your encryption key will be destroyed, making stored data permanently unrecoverable.
